title: Unstated identification Hess(lambda_i)=H^{(i)} across two parametrizations

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Lemma (lem:hess) defines $H^{(i)}=\operatorname{Hess}_{(\alpha,\beta)}\gamma_i$, the Hessian of the ORDERED root map $\Psi_{\boxplus}$. Corollary (cor:psd) works with the hyperbolic eigenvalues $\lambda_i(x,a,b)$ of $f$ in direction $e_1$ and then asserts, at $x=0,a=\alpha,b=\beta$, that 'Hess$\,\lambda_i=H^{(i)}$'. This identification is correct but is never derived.

The missing computation: the roots of $t\mapsto f(x+t,a,b)$ are exactly $t=\gamma_i(a,b)-x$, so $\lambda_i(x,a,b)=\gamma_i(a,b)-x$. Hence the $x$-dependence is affine and the $(a,b)$-block of $\operatorname{Hess}\lambda_i$ equals $H^{(i)}$ at $x=0$. One must also note that the descending order of the $\lambda_i$ matches that of the $\gamma_i$, which is what makes $c_i=\gamma_i$ a correctly-ordered ($c_1\ge\dots\ge c_n$) coefficient sequence for the Abel summation. Without this explicit relation the corollary's evaluation step is unjustified.
